name: Remote: VNC Access from the Internet
notes: This rule triggers when VNC (a remote desktop access application) is detected from the Internet to a local host.  Many companies consider this a policy issue that should be addressed.  If this is normal activity on your network, disable this rule.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: VNC Activity from a Remote Host
conditions_text: when a flow matches all of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: VNC Activity from a Remote Host, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
TD_list: APPLY Remote: VNC Access from the Internet on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches all of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: VNC Activity from a Remote Host, BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication
RED_list: Event name: VNC Access from the Internet

Event description: Detects VNC (a remote desktop access application) from the Internet to a local host.  Many companies consider this a policy issue that should be addressed.  If this is normal activity on your network, disable this rule.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
